Phase-54.NAME: Admin Console Matrix Redesign
Phase-54.summary: Redesign the full admin frontend into the approved KrotPN Matrix style while keeping dense operator workflows, mobile usability, and safe admin actions.

constraints.constraint-1: No admin API shape changes, auth/session behavior changes, MTProto runtime changes, billing backend changes, deploy/install changes, nginx changes, or docker-compose changes.
constraints.constraint-2: Admin surfaces must stay compact and operational, not marketing-style or decorative-first.
constraints.constraint-3: Destructive and disruptive actions must remain confirmation-safe and readable on mobile.

step-1.action: Audit admin route hierarchy and map every page to Matrix compact operator surfaces.
step-1.acceptance: Route-by-route admin UI target matrix exists before edits.

step-2.action: Redesign admin login with Matrix shell, empty credential fields, icon padding safety, and strong focus states.
step-2.acceptance: Login form remains compact, readable, and free of stale default credential hints.

step-3.action: Redesign admin layout/navigation with compact mobile-first access to dashboard, users, devices, MTProto, servers, plans, analytics, and settings.
step-3.acceptance: Navigation fits mobile without horizontal scroll or hidden emergency actions.

step-4.action: Redesign dashboard, KPI rows, health summaries, and system status surfaces with dense Matrix panels.
step-4.acceptance: Critical health and business metrics remain scannable without decorative card bloat.

step-5.action: Redesign users and devices pages with compact search, pagination, filters, and confirmation-safe actions.
step-5.acceptance: Large user lists remain usable without long unstructured scrolling.

step-6.action: Redesign MTProto admin and analytics pages with compact graphs, alert inbox, IP history panels, and runtime metrics.
step-6.acceptance: Secrets stay redacted and admin investigation data remains readable on mobile.

step-7.action: Redesign plans, servers, and analytics routes with Matrix tables, status badges, charts, and protected tariff matrix.
step-7.acceptance: Canonical tariff controls stay non-CRUD unless a later backend phase approves changes.

step-8.action: Create Phase-54 admin smoke covering mobile routes, desktop routes, reduced-motion, canvas visibility, no overlap, and confirmation dialogs.

step-9.action: Run admin frontend build and local route smoke across mobile and desktop viewport contracts; reserve authenticated screenshot proof for clean-deploy/live admin session.
step-9.acceptance: Frontend-admin build and Phase-54 static viewport/route smoke pass locally.

step-10.action: Update MyGRACE docs, module maps, verification evidence, and current status after implementation.
step-10.acceptance: XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff check, and protected deploy/install guard pass.

mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-1: Admin opens /users on a phone: search, filters, pagination, and user rows remain compact above the Matrix background without long unstructured scroll.
mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-2: Admin opens /mtproto analytics: charts, current IPs, alert actions, and runtime metrics stay readable; no scanline/glitch layer hides incident controls.
mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-3: Admin executes a destructive action: confirmation dialog remains focused, keyboard reachable, and visually separated from animated overlays.

route-1.target: Compact Matrix login shell with empty credential fields, icon padding safety, focus-visible state, and no default credential hints.

route-2.target: Operational dashboard with compact KPI grid, service health, quick actions, and route status strip.

route-3.target: Searchable bounded user inventory with counters, pagination, compact rows, and mobile-safe metadata.

route-4.target: Bounded device inventory with search, anti-sharing signals, one-peer actions, and readable confirmation dialog.

route-5.target: Redacted assignment inventory, runtime health, embedded analytics, bounded lists, IP history, alerts, promotion tag controls, and confirmation-safe reissue/revoke.

route-6.target: Compact node/route topology, smaller KPI cards, safe radii, readable forms, and preserved CRUD API behavior.

route-7.target: Readonly canonical tariff matrix preserving KrotPN 1/6/9, 369/693/936 RUB, and no accidental CRUD controls.

route-8.target: Compact payment/subscription/referral summaries and Matrix chart frames.

execution-evidence.result: PASS: frontend-admin TypeScript/Vite build passed; Phase-54 static smoke passed for admin route matrix, compact primitives, mobile route safety, confirmation markers, MTProto redaction/analytics, canonical Plans guard, reduced-motion markers, and protected deploy/install/nginx/docker-compose/runtime isolation.
execution-evidence.protected-surface: No install.sh, deploy, nginx, docker-compose.yml, deploy/mtproto-de-compose.yml, or .env.example diff.

verification-design.gate-1: Phase-54 execute may start only after V-M-010, V-M-037, V-M-047, V-M-058, V-M-068, V-M-070, and V-M-071 include PHASE-54 verification-designed coverage.
verification-design.gate-2: Execution must create scripts/phase54-admin-matrix-ui-smoke.mjs before marking step-8 done-local.
verification-design.gate-3: The smoke must cover admin routes /login, /, /users, /devices, /mtproto, /servers, /plans, and /analytics at 360x740, 390x844, 430x932, 768x1024, and 1440x900.
verification-design.gate-4: The smoke must include reduced-motion proof, Matrix canvas nonblank or controlled static fallback proof, no document horizontal overflow, no obvious text/icon/control overlap, and pointer-safe overlays.
verification-design.gate-5: The admin MTProto section must prove compact assignment inventory, embedded analytics, alert actions, IP history, promotion tag controls, reissue/revoke confirmations, and secret redaction.
verification-design.gate-6: The admin Plans section must preserve the canonical 369/693/936 RUB tariff matrix without enabling canonical CRUD actions or frontend-controlled payment fields.
verification-design.gate-7: Final local Phase-54 evidence must include frontend-admin build, Phase-54 static route/viewport smoke, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff --check, and protected deploy/install/nginx/docker-compose/runtime guard; authenticated screenshot review remains clean-deploy follow-up.
